artPrompt: A wild-eyed human prophet in tattered bone-white robes flung backward mid-rapture, arms spread, dissolving upward into cold gold light as ashen congregant silhouettes are dragged skyward with him in foreshortened streaks. The doomsday clock tower looms against a blood-dark sky, candles snuffing, smoke and embers spiraling around his ecstatic anguished face, kinetic ascension, rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: False Prophet of Whateverville
text:
When False Prophet of Whateverville dies, exile all creatures that died this turn alongside it. The rapture takes everyone, ready or not.
Whenever a Prophecy fails anywhere at the table, you gain 1 life. Being wrong is, for him, a business model.
flavorText: Predicted the end of the world on the 4th, the 11th, the 19th, and "definitely the 26th, for real this time."
